member.summary: Loads the initial values for each control based on the InitPolicy, InitFixField and InitValue attributes.
member.param: Value provider for initializing control values from InitFixField.
member.remarks: The spec states: 'If the value of the initPolicy attribute is undefined or equal to "UseValue" and the initValue attribute is defined then initialize with initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" then attempt to initialize with the value of the tag specified in the initFixField attribute. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" and it is not possible to access the value of the specified fix tag then revert to using initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField", the field is not accessible, and initValue is not defined, then do not initialize.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Collections.ReadOnlyControlCollection.TryUpdateParameterValues(Atdl4net.Model.Collections.ParameterCollection,System.Boolean,System.Collections.Generic.IList{Atdl4net.Validation.ValidationResult}@)
member.summary: Updates the parameter values from the controls in this control collection.
member.param: Collection of parameters to be updated.
If true, this method returns as soon as any error is found; if false, an attempt is made to update all parameter values before the method returns.
If one or more validations fail, this parameter contains a list of ValidationResults; null otherwise.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Collections.ReadOnlyControlCollection.UpdateValuesFromParameters(Atdl4net.Model.Collections.ParameterCollection)
member.summary: Updates the values of each control from its respective parameter.
member.param: Parameter collection.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState
member.summary: Represents the state of an enumerated value within FIX, i.e., one that may have a single value, that maps to, say, Char_t, or one that may have several values, which in FIX is typed as a MultipleStringValue.
member.remarks: During its existence, this type has at times been a class and at other times a struct. It has settled on being a class because its key function is to represent changing state, and mutable structs are seen to be a bad thing. As it is a class, the method is included to provide a means to copy the contents in order to avoid the scenario that two independent entities share a reference to a given EnumState.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.#ctor(System.String[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of with the supplied set of EnumID values.
member.param: Array of EnumID values. The order of this array should match the order of the EnumPair elements within the target parameter.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.#ctor(Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of and copies the data from the supplied EnumState.
member.param: EnumState to copy the initial state from.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.Copy
member.summary: Makes a copy (deep clone) of this EnumState.
member.param: EnumState to update.
member.remarks: This method is provided to allow value type-like semantics for EnumState.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.UpdateFrom(Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState)
member.summary: Updates this EnumState with the state of the supplied EnumState.
member.param: Source EnumState to update this instance from.
member.remarks: Implementation note: there are probably more effective ways to copy data from one BitArray to another that the one taken, but the current implementation ensures that the two EnumStates reference a common set of EnumIDs.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Determines whether the object supplied has the identical state to this instance.
member.param: Object to compare this object to.
member.returns: true if the state of the supplied object is identical to this object instance; false otherwise.
member.remarks: This method assumes that both operands have the same set of EnumID values.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.GetHashCode
member.summary: Serves as a hash function for this type. Overridden because Equals(object) is overridden.
member.returns: A hash code for the current Object.
member.remarks: The value 251 is used here because it is a prime number, helpful for generating unique hash values.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.Item(System.String)
member.summary: Gets/sets the boolean state of the enumerated element specified by the supplied EnumID value.
member.param: EnumID to get/set the boolean state for.
member.returns: Boolean state for the specified EnumID.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of elements (EnumIDs) that this EnumState holds state for.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.IsValidEnumId(System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether the supplied EnumID value is valid for this EnumState instance.
member.param: EnumID value to evaluate.
member.returns: true if the supplied EnumID is valid for this EnumState; false otherwise.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.NonEnumValue
member.summary: Gets/sets the non-enum value; the non-enum value is only used by the EditableDropDownList_t type.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.GetFirstSelectedEnumId
member.summary: Gets the first EnumID that has a value of true.
member.returns: The first EnumID if any enumerated values are set; otherwise an empty string is returned.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.GetFirstSelectedEnumIdIndex
member.summary: Gets the (zero-based) index of the first EnumID that has a value of true.
member.returns: The index of the first EnumID if any enumerated values are set; -1 otherwise.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.GetIndexOfEnumId(System.String)
member.summary: Gets the (zero-based) index of the supplied EnumID.
member.returns: The index of the supplied EnumID if that matches a valid enumerated value identifier; -1 otherwise.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.ClearAll
member.summary: Sets the state of all enumerated values to false.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.LoadInitValue(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Loads the state of this EnumState from the supplied string in MultipleStringValue format, e.g., "EnumId_1 EnumId_2 EnumId_3". (Elements may be separated with space, semi-colon or comma.)
member.param: MultipleStringValue format string containing the EnumIDs that specify the initial state for this EnumState.
If set to true, and initValues contains one or more values that don't correspond to a valid EnumID, then assume that initValues should be treated as the non-enumerated value for this EnumState.
member.remarks: This method should not be confused with , as that method parses the supplied string for FIX wire values whereas this method parses the string for EnumIDs.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.ToWireValue(Atdl4net.Model.Collections.EnumPairCollection)
member.summary: Provides the state of this EnumState in a format ready to be sent over FIX, e.g., "A B C E".
member.param: The EnumPairs for this parameter, to provide the mapping from EnumID values.
member.returns: A space-separated string containing zero or more EnumPair WireValues. If no EnumIDs are enabled, then null is returned.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.FromWireValue(Atdl4net.Model.Collections.EnumPairCollection,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new EnumState from the supplied set of EnumPairs and input FIX string.
member.param: EnumPairs for this parameter.
String containing one or more FIX wire values (space-separated).

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.ToString
member.summary: Provides a string representation of this EnumState. Primarily for debugging purposes.
member.returns: String representation of this EnumState.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.EnumState.CompareTo(System.Object)
member.summary: Compares the current instance with another object of the same type and returns an integer that indicates whether the current instance precedes, follows, or occurs in the same position in the sort order as the other object.
member.remarks: IComparable is implemented by EnumState to allow its use within Edit_t processing. Although this interface is designed to provide less than/greater than comparison, it is primarily used here for determining equality.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.IControlVisitor.Visit(Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t)
member.summary: Method that the visitor will call based on the type of the control parameter.
member.param: Control to process as part of this visitor pattern.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.InitializableControl`1
member.summary: Generic base class for all controls. Provides the ability to initialize controls based on the state of the InitPolicy, InitFixField and InitValue attributes.
member.typeparam: Specified the type of the InitValue. Note that this may not be the same as the type that the control uses to store its data, for example InitValue for list controls is of type string whereas this type of control uses EnumState to store its state.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.InitializableControl`1.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance with the specified identifier as id.
member.param: Id of this control.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.InitializableControl`1.InitValue
member.summary: The value used to pre-populate the GUI component when the order entry screen is initially rendered.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.InitializableControl`1.LoadInitValue(Atdl4net.Fix.FixFieldValueProvider)
member.summary: Loads the initial value for this control based on the InitPolicy, InitFixField and InitValue attributes.
member.param: Value provider for initializing control values from InitFixField.
member.remarks:
The spec states: 'If the value of the initPolicy attribute is undefined or equal to "UseValue" and the initValue attribute is defined then initialize with initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" then attempt to initialize with the value of the tag specified in the initFixField attribute. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" and it is not possible to access the value of the specified fix tag then revert to using initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField", the field is not accessible, and initValue is not defined, then do not initialize.
Note that it is possible to initialize an enumerated control (e.g., DropDownList_t) from a FIX_ value. In this case, it must be possible to retrieve a valid EnumID for the supplied FIX wire value. The target parameter is used to translate the wire value into an EnumID.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.InitializableControl`1.LoadDefaultFromFixValue(System.String)
member.summary: Attempts to load the supplied FIX field value into this control.
member.param: Value to set this control to.
member.returns: true if it was possible to set the value of this control using the supplied value; false otherwise.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.InitializableControl`1.LoadDefaultFromInitValue
member.summary: Loads this control with any supplied InitValue. If InitValue is not supplied, then control value will be set to default/empty value.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.IParameterConvertible
member.summary: Interface that all controls must implement such that their values can be converted to a form accepted by instances.
member.remarks:
If the control's value cannot be converted to the target type, then an is thrown.
(Atdl4net doesn't use as that does not support the conversion to and from Nullable types.)

member.returns: One of true, false or null which is equivalent to the value of this instance.

member.summary: Base class for all concrete types.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance with the specified identifier as id.
member.param: Id of this control.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.DisableForTemplate
member.summary: For implementing systems that support saving order templates or pre-populated orders for basket trading/list trading this attribute specifies that the control should be disabled when the order screen is going to be saved as a template and not actually used to place an order.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.Id
member.summary: Unique identifier of this control. No two controls of the same strategy can have the same ID.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.Index
member.summary: Zero-based index for this control within a StrategyPanel_t. For example, if a StrategyPanel_t has three controls, the first would have index of 0, the second 1 and the third 2.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.InitFixField
member.summary: Indicates the initialization value is to be taken from this standard FIX field. Format: "FIX_" + FIXFieldName. E.g. "FIX_OrderQty". Required when initPolicy=”UseFixField”.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.InitPolicy
member.summary: Describes how to initialize the control. If the value of this attribute is undefined or equal to "UseValue" and initValue is defined then initialize with initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" then attempt to initialize with the value of the tag specified in initFixField. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" and it is not possible to access the value of the specified fix tag then revert to using initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField", the field is not accessible, and initValue is not defined, then do not initialize.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.Label
member.summary: A title for this control which may be displayed.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.ParameterRef
member.summary: The name of the parameter for which this control gives the visual representation. A parameter with this name must be defined within the same strategy as this control.
member.remarks: The property provides access to the parameter instance itself, whilst this property provides access to the name of the parameter.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.ToolTip
member.summary: Tool tip text for rendered GUI objects rendered for the parameter.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.IsToggleable
member.summary: Indicates whether this control can be toggled (i.e., is a checkbox or radiobutton).

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.OwningStrategyPanel
member.summary: Gets the StrategyPanel that this control belongs to.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.SetValue(System.Object)
member.summary: Sets the value of this control; either with a value of the appropriate type, or using the FIXatdl '{NULL}' value. This method is either called indirectly from the user interface, or by a StateRule.
member.param: Either a valid instance of the appropriate type or null (meaning do not send this value over FIX). May also contain the FIXatdl '{NULL}' value as a string.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.Reset
member.summary: Resets this control to either a null value or for list controls, all options unselected.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.GetValueForParameter
member.summary: Provides access to the value of this control using the interface which means that this control's value can be converted into a form that is appropriate for the target parameter.
member.returns: An object through which the control's value can be accessed.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.StateRules
member.summary: Gets the collection of s for this control.

member.summary: Adds support for the visitor pattern, enabling the appropriate Visit() method to be called on the visitor depending on its concrete type.
member.param: Visitor.

member.summary: Gets the current value of this control, for use in Edits as part of StateRules but also used internally in the View Model.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.SetValueFromParameter(Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter)
member.summary: Sets the value of this control using the value of the supplied parameter.
member.param: Parameter to set this control's value from.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t.LoadInitValue(Atdl4net.Fix.FixFieldValueProvider)
member.summary: Loads the initial value for this control based on the InitPolicy, InitFixField and InitValue attributes.
member.param: Value provider for initializing control values from InitFixField.
member.remarks: The spec states: 'If the value of the initPolicy attribute is undefined or equal to "UseValue" and the initValue attribute is defined then initialize with initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" then attempt to initialize with the value of the tag specified in the initFixField attribute. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" and it is not possible to access the value of the specified fix tag then revert to using initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField", the field is not accessible, and initValue is not defined, then do not initialize.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t
member.summary: Represents the FIXatdl type Edit_t when it occurs outside of a StateRule_t or a StrategyEdit_t element.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t.Field
member.summary: Gets/sets the first field name for comparison. When the edit is used within a StateRule, this field must refer to the ID of a Control. When the edit is used within a StrategyEdit, this field must refer to either the name of a parameter or a standard FIX field name. When referring to a standard FIX tag then the name must be pre-pended with the string "FIX_", e.g. "FIX_OrderQty". Required the Operator is not null.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t.Field2
member.summary: Gets/sets the optional second field name for comparison. When the edit is used within a StateRule, this field must refer to the ID of a Control. When the edit is used within a StrategyEdit, this field must refer to either the name of a parameter or a standard FIX field name. When referring to a standard FIX tag then the name must be pre-pended with the string "FIX_", e.g. "FIX_OrderQty".

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t`1
member.summary: Represents a FIXatdl Edit_t when implemented within a StateRule_t or StrategyEdit_t element.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t`1.ToString
member.summary: Provides a string representation of this Edit_t, primarily for debugging purposes.

member.summary: Gets the set of sources for this Edit and its children. As source is non-null Field or Field2 value.

member.summary: Gets/sets the name of field to be used as left hand side of the evaluation.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t`1.Field2
member.summary: Gets/sets the name of second (optional) field, to be used as the right hand side of the evaluation.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t`1.Id
member.summary: Gets/sets the optional ID for this Edit.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t`1.Operator
member.summary: Gets/sets the optional operator - used when comparing two values.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Edit_t`1.Value
member.summary: Gets/sets the optional fixed value to be used as the right hand side of the evaluation.
member.remarks:
From the spec:
"When Edit is a descendant of a StateRule element, Value refers to the value of the control referred by Field. If the control referred by Field has enumerated values then Value refers to the enumID of one of the control's ListItem elements.
When Edit is a descendant of a StrategyEdit element, Value refers to the wireValue of the parameter referred by Field."

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Market_t
member.summary:
Represents a FIXatdl Market_t.
Defines a particular market using a market identifier code (MIC). An attribute, inclusion, determines whether the market should be included or excluded from the list of markets created by the patterned element, Markets.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Market_t.Inclusion
member.summary: Indicates whether this market should be included or excluded from encompassing list. Valid values: are “Include”, “Exclude”.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Market_t.MICCode
member.summary: String representing a market or exchange - ISO 10383 Market Identifier Code (MIC).

member.summary: The underlying value of this parameter.

member.summary: Value accessor. This property is intended to be used for deserialization purposes only.

member.summary: Initializes this parameter's value to a newly created instance of the type parameter. This method is used if the parameter is being used within a strategy subsequent to its creation, say for an order amendment.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.DefinedByFix
member.summary: Gets/sets the DefinedByFIX property, which indicates whether the parameter is a redefinition of a standard FIX tag. The default value is false.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.EnumPairs
member.summary: Gets/sets the enum pairs for this parameter. Although it doesn't necessarily make sense in all cases, all parameter types within FIXatdl may contain an EnumPairs element, so we must support it at the base level.
member.value: The enum pairs. Will be an empty collection if no enum pairs were present in the parameter definition.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.HasEnumPairs
member.summary: Indicates whether the parameter has an EnumPairs element with at least one sub-element.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.FixTag
member.summary: Gets or sets the FIX tag for this parameter, i.e., the tag that will hold the value of the parameter. Required when parameter value is intended to be transported over the wire. If fixTag is not provided then the Strategies-level attribute, tag957Support, must be set to true, indicating that the order recipient expects to receive algo parameters in the StrategyParameterGrp repeating group beginning at tag 957. NB Atdl4net does not currently support usage of the StrategyParameterGrp element.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.MutableOnCxlRpl
member.summary: Indicates whether this parameter’s value can be modified by an Order Cancel/Replace Request message. The default value for this field is true.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.Name
member.remarks: No two parameters of any strategy may have the same name. The name may be used as a unique key when referenced from the other sub-schemas. Names must begin with an alpha character followed only by alpha-numeric characters and must not contain whitespace characters.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.RevertOnCxlRpl
member.summary: Indicates how to interpret those tags that were populated in an original order but are not populated in a subsequent cancel/replace of the order message. If this value is true then revert to the value of the original order, otherwise a null value or the parameter’s default value (Control/@initValue) is to be used or if none is specified, the parameter is to be omitted. The default value for this field is false.
member.remarks: Although revertOnCxlRpl and mutableOnCxlRpl might appear to be mutually exclusive, this is not strictly the case, and as the default value for mutableOnCxlRpl is 'true', it is recommended practice to explicitly include mutableOnCxlRpl="false" if the option revertOnCxlRpl="true" is set for a given parameter (assuming of course this is the intended behaviour).

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the type name of this parameter.
member.value: The type name (one of Amt_t, Boolean_t, Char_t, etc.).

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.Use
member.summary: Indicates whether a parameter is optional or required. Valid values are "optional" and "required". The default value for this field is "optional".

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Parameter_t`1.IsSet
member.summary: Indicates whether this parameter has been set to a value other than null.

member.summary: Gets the value of this parameter as seen by the Control_t that references it. May be null if the parameter has no value, for example if it has explicitly been set via a state rule to {NULL}.
member.remarks: An is returned enabling the parameter value to be converted into any desired type, provided that the underlying value supports that type.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.StrategyEdit_t
member.summary: Represents the FIXatdl StrategyEdit element, which is a definition of a validation rule. A StrategyEdit element must contain an Edit element as a child. The boolean expression described by the Edit element is an assertion, i.e., validation succeeds if the condition described by the Edit is true and fails when the condition described by the Edit element is false. In the case where validation fails, the error message, supplied by the errorMsg attribute of the StrategyEdit, may be displayed to an OMS user or logged.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.StrategyEdit_t.InternalId
member.summary: Gets the internal ID for this StrategyEdit; used to support lookups when applying the results of validations to controls.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.StrategyEdit_t.ErrorMessage
member.summary: Gets/sets the error message to display when the boolean expression defined by StrategyEdit/Edit evaluates to False.

member.summary: Represents the FIXatdl StrategyLayout element that contains the root StrategyPanel.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.DisclosureDoc

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.FixMsgType
member.summary: Gets/sets the FIX message to use when transmitting the order that this Strategy relates to. Values are taken from FIX tag 35 and may be one of "D" (NewOrder-Single), "E" (NewOrder-List), "AB" (NewOrder-Multileg), or "s" (NewOrder-Cross).

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.ImageLocation
member.summary: Gets/sets the file path or URL of an image file or logo for this particular strategy.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.Markets
member.summary:
This element defines the markets/exchanges (by ISO 10383 MIC Code) to which the strategy is applicable. If no Markets element is defined then the strategy is applicable for ALL markets. If a market is defined and has its 'inclusion' attribute set to "Include", then it is implied that the strategy is applicable for ONLY that market. If a market is defined and is set to "Exclude", then it is implied that the strategy is applicable for all markets EXCEPT that market.
Include takes precedence over Exclude - for example, if XNAS is defined and set to "Include" and XLON is defined and set to "Exclude" then all other markets will also be excluded since the "Include" on XNAS takes precedence over the "Exclude" on XLON. In this example, the definition of XLON as "Exclude" is unnecessary. Markets are used in conjunction with regions and countries to define the scope of the strategy. Markets take precedence over regions and countries. For example, if AsiaPacificJapan is defined as "Exclude" but the Fukuoka Stock Exchange (XFKA) is defined as an included market, the strategy will be applicable for all markets in The Americas and EMEA, as well as only the Fukuoka Stock Exchange in the APAC region.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.Name
member.summary: Gets/sets the unique identifier of a Strategy. Strategy names must be unique per provider.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.OrderSequenceTag
member.summary: Gets/sets the tag which contains the sequence number of a particular order of a basket.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.Parameters
member.summary: Gets the collection of Parameters for this Strategy.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.ProviderId
member.summary: Gets/sets a string that identifies the firm providing the algorithm.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.ProviderSubId
member.summary: Gets/sets a string that provides a further level of firm identification.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.Regions
member.summary: Gets the Regions that this Strategy pertains to.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.RepeatingGroup
member.summary: Gets/sets the group of Parameter elements that are intended for use with multi-leg or basket strategies. Parameters contained within a RepeatingGroup element are intended to have their tag=value pairs populated in either the ListOrdGrp repeating group of a New Order List message or the LegOrdGrp repeating group of a New Order Multileg message. Parameters not contained within a RepeatingGroup element have their values populated in the main body of a message.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.SecurityTypes
member.summary: Gets the list of security types (by SecurityType (tag 167)) for which this Strategy is valid. The absence of any security types implies that the strategy is valid for all security types.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.SentOrderLink
member.summary: Gets/sets the prefix portion of a URL used to access the order or draft at the target e.g. https://xyz.com/algo/dashboard?SenderCompID= - an OMS can append to this the specific SenderCompID string, an ampersand "ClOrdID=" and the specific ClOrdID-string. Trader hits this full URL to communicate regarding the order or draft. See additional documentation.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.StrategyEdits
member.summary: Gets the collection of StrategyEdits for validating the output of this Strategy.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.StrategyLayout
member.summary: Gets/sets the StrategyLayout for this Strategy, which itself contains the root StrategyPanel for displaying the Strategy.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.TotalLegs
member.summary: Gets/sets a field that denotes number of repeating legs; used when msgType is AB.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.TotalOrders
member.summary: Gets/sets a field that denotes the number of repeating orders in a NewOrder-List message or a basket of NewOrder-Single messages.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.UiRep
member.summary: Gets/sets the name of the strategy as rendered in the user interface (UI). If not provided then the "name" attribute should be used. (This is the value rendered on the UI when the user is presented with a choice of algorithms.)

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.Version
member.summary: Gets/sets information to facilitate version control

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.WireValue
member.summary: Gets/sets the value used to identify the algorithm. The tag referred to by at the Strategies level within the FIXatdl file will be set to this value.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.LoadInitialControlValues(Atdl4net.Fix.FixFieldValueProvider)
member.summary: Loads the initial control values, either from the control's initValue attribute, or using the FIX_ mechanism in conjunction with the supplied initial FIX values.
member.param: Initial value provider that provides access to the initial FIX field values, needed to allow control values to be initialised using the FIX_ mechanism .

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.UpdateControlValuesFromParameters(Atdl4net.Fix.FixFieldValueProvider)
member.summary: Updates the values of each control within this strategy from its respective parameter.
member.param: Initial value provider that provides access to the initial FIX field values, needed to allow control values to be initialised using the FIX_ mechanism .

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.LoadParameterValues(Atdl4net.Fix.FixFieldValueProvider,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Loads this strategy's parameters with the supplied FIX values.
member.param: providing the FIX values to initialize from.
Set to true if each parameter value is to be reset if its value is specified in inputValues; set to false to leave the parameter value unchanged.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.RunAllStateRules
member.summary: Evaluates all state rules for all strategies.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.EvaluateAllStrategyEdits(Atdl4net.Fix.IInitialFixValueProvider,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Evaluate all the StrategyEdits for this strategy.
member.param: Provider that providers access to any additional FIX field values that may be required in the Edit evaluation.
If true, this method returns as soon as any error is found; if false, all StrategyEdits are evaluated before the method returns.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.TryUpdateParameterValuesFromControls(System.Boolean,System.Collections.Generic.IList{Atdl4net.Validation.ValidationResult}@)
member.summary: Attempts to updates the parameter values from the controls in this strategy.
member.param: If true, this method returns as soon as any error is found; if false, an attempt is made to update all parameter values before the method returns.
If one or more validations fail, this parameter contains a list of ValidationResults; null otherwise.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Strategy_t.Reset

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter
member.summary: Common interface for all Parameter_t types. This interface has members for all the attributes of the FIXatdl Parameter_t type that are common across all FIXatdl types (Amt_t, Boolean_t, etc.), allowing each parameter to be treated as a common type (IParameter) regardless of the type parameter used to create the for the FIXatdl parameter.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.Reset
member.summary: Initializes this parameter's value to a newly created instance of the type parameter. This method is used if the parameter is being used within a strategy subsequent to its creation, say for an order amendment.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.DefinedByFix
member.summary: Gets/sets the DefinedByFIX property, which indicates whether the parameter is a redefinition of a standard FIX tag. The default value is false.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.EnumPairs
member.summary: Gets/sets the enum pairs for this parameter. Although it doesn't necessarily make sense in all cases, all parameter types within FIXatdl may contain an EnumPairs element, so we must support it at the base level.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.HasEnumPairs
member.summary: Indicates whether the parameter has an EnumPairs element with at least one sub-element.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.FixTag
member.summary: Gets or sets the FIX tag for this parameter, i.e., the tag that will hold the value of the parameter. Required when parameter value is intended to be transported over the wire. If fixTag is not provided then the Strategies-level attribute, tag957Support, must be set to true, indicating that the order recipient expects to receive algo parameters in the StrategyParameterGrp repeating group beginning at tag 957. NB Atdl4net does not currently support usage of the StrategyParameterGrp element.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.MutableOnCxlRpl
member.summary: Indicates whether this parameter�s value can be modified by an Order Cancel/Replace Request message. The default value for this field is true.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.Name
member.remarks: No two parameters of any strategy may have the same name. The name may be used as a unique key when referenced from the other sub-schemas. Names must begin with an alpha character followed only by alpha-numeric characters and must not contain whitespace characters.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.RevertOnCxlRpl
member.summary: Indicates how to interpret those tags that were populated in an original order but are not populated in a subsequent cancel/replace of the order message. If this value is true then revert to the value of the original order, otherwise a null value or the parameter�s default value (Control/@initValue) is to be used or if none is specified, the parameter is to be omitted. The default value for this field is false.
member.remarks: Although revertOnCxlRpl and mutableOnCxlRpl might appear to be mutually exclusive, this is not strictly the case, and as the default value for mutableOnCxlRpl is 'true', it is recommended practice to explicitly include mutableOnCxlRpl="false" if the option revertOnCxlRpl="true" is set for a given parameter (assuming of course this is the intended behaviour).

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.Type
member.summary: Gets or sets the type name of this parameter.
member.value: The type name (one of Amt_t, Boolean_t, Char_t, etc.).

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.Use
member.summary: Indicates whether a parameter is optional or required. Valid values are "optional" and "required". The default value for this field is "optional".

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.GetValueForControl
member.summary: Gets this parameter's value in a form suitable for populating its respective control.
member.returns: An that can be used to convert this parameter into the appropriate form for the target control.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.SetValueFromControl(Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Control_t)
member.summary: Sets the value of this parameter using the Control_t that references it. The resulting parameter value may be null if the control is not set to a value, or if it has explicitly been set via a state rule to {NULL}.
member.param: Control to extract this parameter's value from.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.WireValue
member.summary: Gets/sets the wire value of this parameter.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter.IsSet
member.summary: Indicates whether this parameter has been set to a value other than null.

member.summary: No border is to be shown.

member.summary: A line border is to be shown.

member.summary: Used with single spinner control, defines how to determine the increment.

member.name: F:Atdl4net.Model.Enumerations.IncrementPolicy_t.LotSize
member.summary: Use the round lot size of symbol.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Enumerations.InitPolicy_t
member.summary: Describes how to initialize a control. If the value of this attribute is undefined or equal to "UseValue" and initValue is defined then initialize with initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" then attempt to initialize with the value of the tag specified in initFixField. If the value is equal to "UseFixField" and it is not possible to access the value of the specified fix tag then revert to using initValue. If the value is equal to "UseFixField", the field is not accessible, and initValue is not defined, then do not initialize.

member.name: F:Atdl4net.Model.Enumerations.InitPolicy_t.UseValue
member.summary: If initValue is defined then initialize with initValue.

member.name: F:Atdl4net.Model.Enumerations.InitPolicy_t.UseFixField
member.summary: Attempt to initialize with the value of the tag specified in initFixField.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Amt_t
member.summary: Represents a floating point field typically representing a Price times a Qty.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Boolean_t
member.summary: Represents a char field containing one of two values: "Y" = True/Yes; "N" = False/No.'

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Boolean_t.FalseWireValue
member.summary:
Gets or sets the false wire value (for use with Boolean type parameters).
This attribute is targeted for deprecation.
Defines the value with which to populate the FIX message when the boolean parameter is False. Overrides the standard FIX boolean value of “N”. I.e. if this attribute is not provided then the order-sending application must use “N”.
If it is desired that the FIX message is not to be populated with this tag when the value of the parameter is false, then falseWireValue should be defined as “{NULL}”.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Boolean_t.TrueWireValue
member.summary: Applicable only when xsi:type is Boolean_t. This attribute is targeted for deprecation. To achieve the same functionality, it is recommended that a Char_t or String_t type parameter be used instead of a Boolean_t. The parameter should have two EnumPairs defined with one defining the false wire-value and the other defining the true wire-value. The parameter should be bound to a CheckBox control. The CheckBox control should define the parameters checkedEnumRef and uncheckedEnumRef to refer to the enumIDs of the parameter. See the section “A Sample FIXatdl Document” in this document for an example. (See the section “A Sample FIXatdl Document” in this document for an example. Examine the Parameter “AllowDarkPoolExec” and Control “DPOption” for details.) The deprecated use is described as follows: Defines the value with which to populate the FIX message when the boolean parameter is True. Overrides the standard FIX boolean value of “Y”. I.e. if this attribute is not provided then the order-sending application must use “Y”. If it is desired that the FIX message is not to be populated with this tag when the value of the parameter is true, then trueWireValue should be defined as “{NULL}”.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Boolean_t.ValidateValue(System.Nullable{System.Boolean},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Validates the supplied value in terms of the parameters constraints. This method does nothing because is not possible for a boolean value to be invalid.
member.param: Value to validate, may be null in which case no validation is applied.
Set to true to check that this parameter is non-null.
member.returns: ValidationResult indicating whether the supplied value is valid.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Boolean_t.ConvertFromWireValueFormat(System.String)
member.summary: Converts the supplied value from string format (as might be used on the FIX wire) into the type of the type parameter for this type.
member.param: Type to convert from string; cannot be null as empty fields are invalid in FIX.
member.returns: Value converted from a string.

member.summary: Converts the supplied value to a string, as might be used on the FIX wire.
member.param: Value to convert, may be null.
member.returns: If input value is not null, returns value converted to a string; null otherwise.

member.remarks: Arguably this type of value should be stored as a byte rather than char, as FIX is an ASCII protocol. However, this would mean converting from char to byte when working with strings, which isn't really desirable.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Data_t
member.summary: Represents a string field containing raw data with no format or content restrictions. Data fields are always immediately preceded by a length field. The length field should specify the number of bytes of the value of the data field (up to but not including the terminating SOH). Caution: the value of one of these fields may contain the delimiter (SOH) character. Note that the value specified for this field should be followed by the delimiter (SOH) character as all fields are terminated with an "SOH".
member.remarks: As there is no way within FIXatdl of associating one field with another to provide the length, Data_t fields do not appear to be particularly useful; they are implemented within Atdl4net for completeness.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Float_t
member.summary: Represents a sequence of digits with optional decimal point and sign character (ASCII characters "-", "0" - "9" and "."); the absence of the decimal point within the string will be interpreted as the float representation of an integer value. All float fields must accommodate up to fifteen significant digits. The number of decimal places used should be a factor of business/market needs and mutual agreement between counterparties. Note that float values may contain leading zeros (e.g. "00023.23" = "23.23") and may contain or omit trailing zeros after the decimal point (e.g. "23.0" = "23.0000" = "23" = "23."). Note that fields which are derived from float may contain negative values unless explicitly specified otherwise.'

member.remarks: When converting from DateTime to string, the first member of the returned array is used. When converting from string to DateTime, the member of the array that has the same length as the string value is used.

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Types.MultipleCharValue_t.InvertOnWire
member.summary:
Gets or sets the invert on wire property.
Applicable when: xsi:type is MultipleStringValue_t or MultipleCharValue_t. Instructs the OMS whether to perform a bitwise “not” operation on each element of these lists.
member.value: true to instruct the OMS to invert; false otherwise.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.NumInGroup_t
member.summary: NumInGroup_t is used when describing the number of entries in a FIX repeating group.
member.remarks: The FIX specification (5.0 SP2) describes this type as follows: 'int field representing the number of entries in a repeating group. Value must be positive.'

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t
member.summary: 'float field representing a percentage (e.g. 0.05 represents 5% and 0.9525 represents 95.25%). Note the number of decimal places may vary.'

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t.MultiplyBy100
member.summary: Applicable for xsi:type of Percentage_t. If true then percent values must be multiplied by 100 before being sent on the wire. For example, if multiplyBy100 were false then the percentage, 75%, would be sent as 0.75 on the wire. However, if multiplyBy100 were true then 75 would be sent on the wire. If not provided it should be interpreted as false. Use of this attribute is not recommended. The motivation for this attribute is to maximize compatibility with algorithmic interfaces that are non-compliant with FIX in regard to their handling of percentages. In these cases an integer parameter should be used instead of a percentage.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t.ValidateValue(System.Nullable{System.Decimal},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Validates the supplied value in terms of the parameters constraints (e.g., MinValue, MaxValue, etc.).
member.param: Value to validate, may be null in which case no validation is applied.
Set to true to check that this parameter is non-null.
member.returns: ValidationResult indicating whether the supplied value is valid.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t.ConvertFromWireValueFormat(System.String)
member.summary: Converts the supplied value from string format (as might be used on the FIX wire) into the type of the type parameter for this type. This implementation adjusts for the fact that percentage values are typically shown as whole numbers (5, 10, 15) on the user interface but sent over the FIX wire as decimals (0.05, 0.1, 0.15).
member.param: Type to convert from string; cannot be null as empty fields are invalid in FIX.
member.returns: Value converted from a string.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t.ConvertToWireValueFormat(System.Nullable{System.Decimal})
member.summary: Converts the supplied value to a string, as might be used on the FIX wire. If the supplied value is null, this means the field is not to be included in the outgoing FIX message. This implementation adjusts for the fact that percentage values are typically shown as whole numbers (5, 10, 15) on the user interface but sent over the FIX wire as decimals (0.05, 0.1, 0.15).
member.param: Value to convert, may be null.
member.returns: If input value is not null, returns value converted to a string; null otherwise.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t.ConvertToNativeType(Atdl4net.Model.Elements.Support.IParameter,Atdl4net.Model.Controls.Support.IParameterConvertible)
member.summary: Converts the supplied value to the type parameter type (T?) for this class.
member.param: Parameter that this value belongs to.
Value to convert, may be null.
member.returns: If input value is not null, returns value converted to T?; null otherwise.
member.remarks:
Used when setting a parameter value from a control (or anything else that implements ).
Unlike all other (non-enumerated) control/parameter relationships, Percentage_t does not have a one-to-one mapping with its associated control value as the control will typically contain a user-oriented format (e.g., 25) when the parameter must contain the true value (i.e., 0.25, assuming multiplyBy100 is not set to true).

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t.GetNativeValue(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets the value of this parameter type in its native (i.e., raw) form, such as int, char, string, etc.
member.param: If set to true, the value returned is adjusted to be in the 'format' it would be if sent on the FIX wire. In this case, we have to apply both Precision and the MultiplyBy100 flag.
member.returns: Native parameter value.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Percentage_t.ToDecimal

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.PriceOffset_t
member.summary: 'float field representing a price offset, which can be mathematically added to a "Price". Note the number of decimal places may vary and some fields such as LastForwardPoints may be negative.'

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Price_t
member.summary: 'float field representing a price. Note the number of decimal places may vary. For certain asset classes prices may be negative values. For example, prices for options strategies can be negative under certain market conditions. Refer to Volume 7: FIX Usage by Product for asset classes that support negative price values.'

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Qty_t
member.summary: 'float field capable of storing either a whole number (no decimal places) of "shares" (securities denominated in whole units) or a decimal value containing decimal places for non-share quantity asset classes (securities denominated in fractional units).'

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.SeqNum_t
member.summary: 'int field representing a message sequence number. Value must be positive.'

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.String_t
member.summary: Represents an alpha-numeric free format strings, can include any character or punctuation except the delimiter. All String fields are case sensitive (i.e. morstatt != Morstatt).

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Support.AtdlReferenceType`1
member.summary: Base class for all reference type parameters (String_t, MultipleCharValue_t, MultipleStringValue_t, etc.).
member.remarks: Parameter types must be one of or . The reason for the differentiation is that most FIXatdl types that use value types for the underlying storage (Int_t, Float_t, UTCTimestamp_t, etc.) actually use so that they can also contain null, meaning don't include this value in the FIX output. However, Nullable<T> is a value type, not a reference type, and so a different base type is required to support underlying reference type usage, such as in String_t. (This is the same reason that it isn't possible to factor out apparently duplicated code across AtdlValueType<T> and AtdlReferenceType<T>, because one uses T? internally and the other uses T.)

member.summary: Gets the DateTime format strings to use when converting this date/time to a FIX string and vice versa.
member.returns: Format strings suitable when calling DateTime.ToString(). At least one format string will be returned.
member.remarks: When converting from DateTime to string, the first member of the returned array is used. When converting from string to DateTime, the member of the array that has the same length as the string value is used.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.Tenor_t
member.summary: 'used to allow the expression of FX standard tenors in addition to the base valid enumerations defined for the field that uses this pattern data type. This pattern data type is defined as follows: Dx = tenor expression for "days", e.g. "D5", where "x" is any integer > 0 Mx = tenor expression for "months", e.g. "M3", where "x" is any integer > 0 Wx = tenor expression for "weeks", e.g. "W13", where "x" is any integer > 0 Yx = tenor expression for "years", e.g. "Y1", where "x" is any integer > 0'

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.TZTimeOnly_t
member.summary: 'string field representing the time represented based on ISO 8601. This is the time with a UTC offset to allow identification of local time and timezone of that time. Format is HH:MM[:SS][Z | [ + | - hh[:mm]]] where HH = 00-23 hours, MM = 00-59 minutes, SS = 00-59 seconds, hh = 01-12 offset hours, mm = 00-59 offset minutes. Example: 07:39Z is 07:39 UTC Example: 02:39-05 is five hours behind UTC, thus Eastern Time Example: 15:39+08 is eight hours ahead of UTC, Hong Kong/Singapore time Example: 13:09+05:30 is 5.5 hours ahead of UTC, India time'

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.TZTimeOnly_t.GetDateTimeFormatStrings
member.summary: Gets the DateTime format strings to use when converting this date/time to a FIX string and vice versa.
member.returns: Format strings suitable when calling DateTime.ToString().
member.remarks: When converting from DateTime to string, the first member of the returned array is used. When converting from string to DateTime, the member of the array that has the same length as the string value is used.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.TZTimeOnly_t.GetHumanReadableTypeName
member.summary: Gets the human-readable type name for use in error messages shown to the user.
member.returns: Human-readable type name.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.TZTimestamp_t
member.summary: 'string field representing a time/date combination representing local time with an offset to UTC to allow identification of local time and timezone offset of that time. The representation is based on ISO 8601. Format is YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS[Z | [ + | - hh[:mm]]] where YYYY = 0000 to 9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31 HH = 00-23 hours, MM = 00-59 minutes, SS = 00-59 seconds, hh = 01-12 offset hours, mm = 00-59 offset minutes Example: 20060901-07:39Z is 07:39 UTC on 1st of September 2006 Example: 20060901-02:39-05 is five hours behind UTC, thus Eastern Time on 1st of September 2006 Example: 20060901-15:39+08 is eight hours ahead of UTC, Hong Kong/Singapore time on 1st of September 2006 Example: 20060901-13:09+05:30 is 5.5 hours ahead of UTC, India time on 1st of September 2006'

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.TZTimestamp_t.GetDateTimeFormatStrings
member.summary: Gets the DateTime format strings to use when converting this date/time to a FIX string and vice versa.
member.returns: Format strings suitable when calling DateTime.ToString().
member.remarks: When converting from DateTime to string, the first member of the returned array is used. When converting from string to DateTime, the member of the array that has the same length as the string value is used.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.TZTimestamp_t.GetHumanReadableTypeName
member.summary: Gets the human-readable type name for use in error messages shown to the user.
member.returns: Human-readable type name.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCDateOnly_t
member.summary: 'string field representing Date represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in YYYYMMDD format. This special-purpose field is paired with UTCTimeOnly to form a proper UTCTimestamp for bandwidth-sensitive messages. Valid values: YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31.'
member.remarks: Currently, UTCDateOnly_t does NOT inherit from UTCDateTimeBase as there is no reason to apply any conversions to a date-only field.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCDateOnly_t.GetDateTimeFormatStrings
member.summary: Gets the DateTime format strings to use when converting this date/time to a FIX string and vice versa.
member.returns: Format strings suitable when calling DateTime.ToString().
member.remarks: When converting from DateTime to string, the first member of the returned array is used. When converting from string to DateTime, the member of the array that has the same length as the string value is used.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCDateOnly_t.GetHumanReadableTypeName
member.summary: Gets the human-readable type name for use in error messages shown to the user.
member.returns: Human-readable type name.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCTimeOnly_t
member.summary: 'string field representing Time-only represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either HH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, and period required. This special-purpose field is paired with UTCDateOnly to form a proper UTCTimestamp for bandwidth-sensitive messages. Valid values: HH = 00-23, MM = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second), SS = 00-59. (without milliseconds) HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second), sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds).'

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCTimeOnly_t.GetDateTimeFormatStrings
member.summary: Gets the DateTime format strings to use when converting this date/time to a FIX string and vice versa.
member.returns: Format strings suitable when calling DateTime.ToString().
member.remarks: When converting from DateTime to string, the first member of the returned array is used. When converting from string to DateTime, the member of the array that has the same length as the string value is used.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCTimeOnly_t.GetHumanReadableTypeName
member.summary: Gets the human-readable type name for use in error messages shown to the user.
member.returns: Human-readable type name.

member.name: T:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCTimestamp_t
member.summary: 'string field representing Time/date combination represented in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") in either YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS (whole seconds) or YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss (milliseconds) format, colons, dash, and period required. Valid values: * YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second) (without milliseconds). * YYYY = 0000-9999, MM = 01-12, DD = 01-31, HH = 00-23, MM = 00-59, SS = 00-60 (60 only if UTC leap second), sss=000-999 (indicating milliseconds). Leap Seconds: Note that UTC includes corrections for leap seconds, which are inserted to account for slowing of the rotation of the earth. Leap second insertion is declared by the International Earth Rotation Service (IERS) and has, since 1972, only occurred on the night of Dec. 31 or Jun 30. The IERS considers March 31 and September 30 as secondary dates for leap second insertion, but has never utilized these dates. During a leap second insertion, a UTCTimestamp field may read "19981231-23:59:59", "19981231-23:59:60", "19990101-00:00:00". (see http://tycho.usno.navy.mil/leapsec.html)'

member.name: P:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCTimestamp_t.LocalMktTz
member.summary:
Gets or sets the local market timezone.
Describes the time zone without indicating whether daylight savings is in effect. Valid values are taken from names in the Olson time zone database. All are of the form Area/Location, where Area is the name of a continent or ocean, and Location is the name of a specific location within that region. E.g. Americas/Chicago. Applicable when xsi:type is UTCTimestamp_t.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCTimestamp_t.GetDateTimeFormatStrings
member.summary: Gets the DateTime format strings to use when converting this date/time to a FIX string and vice versa.
member.returns: Format strings suitable when calling DateTime.ToString().
member.remarks: When converting from DateTime to string, the first member of the returned array is used. When converting from string to DateTime, the member of the array that has the same length as the string value is used.

member.name: M:Atdl4net.Model.Types.UTCTimestamp_t.GetHumanReadableTypeName
member.summary: Gets the human-readable type name for use in error messages shown to the user.
member.returns: Human-readable type name.
